# ============================================================
# CELL 1 — Imports & Setup
# ============================================================
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import glob

sns.set_theme()
print('Imports done.')

Imports done.
In [8]:
# ============================================================
# CELL 2 — Load Cleaned Data
# ============================================================

# Metadata
df = pd.read_csv('data/cleaned_data/metadata_cleaned.csv')

# Discharge
discharge_df = pd.read_csv('data/cleaned_data/discharge_cleaned.csv')

# Impedance
impedance_df = pd.read_csv('data/cleaned_data/impedance_cleaned.csv')

# Charge — merge all chunks
charge_files = sorted(glob.glob('data/cleaned_data/charge_chunks/*.csv'))
charge_df = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in charge_files], ignore_index=True)

print('=== LOADED SHAPES ===')
print(f'metadata     : {df.shape}')
print(f'discharge_df : {discharge_df.shape}')
print(f'impedance_df : {impedance_df.shape}')
print(f'charge_df    : {charge_df.shape}')

=== LOADED SHAPES ===
metadata     : (7512, 10)
discharge_df : (764674, 11)
impedance_df : (75376, 14)
charge_df    : (6512619, 11)
In [9]:
# ============================================================
# SCATTER PLOT 1 — Without Hue
# Voltage vs Temperature during Discharge
# Shows the physical relationship between voltage drop
# and heat generated as battery discharges
# ============================================================

# Sample to avoid overplotting 764K points
sample = discharge_df.sample(n=10000, random_state=42)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    data=sample,
    x='Voltage_measured',
    y='Temperature_measured',
    alpha=0.4,
    s=10,
    color='steelblue'
)
plt.title('Discharge: Voltage vs Temperature (no hue)')
plt.xlabel('Voltage Measured (V)')
plt.ylabel('Temperature Measured (°C)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [10]:
# ============================================================
# SCATTER PLOT 2 — With Hue (ambient_temperature)
# Same plot but colored by test temperature group
# Shows how 4°C vs 24°C batteries behave differently
# ============================================================

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    data=sample,
    x='Voltage_measured',
    y='Temperature_measured',
    hue='ambient_temperature',
    palette='coolwarm',
    alpha=0.4,
    s=10
)
plt.title('Discharge: Voltage vs Temperature (hue = ambient temperature)')
plt.xlabel('Voltage Measured (V)')
plt.ylabel('Temperature Measured (°C)')
plt.legend(title='Ambient Temp (°C)', loc='upper right')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [11]:
# ============================================================
# SCATTER PLOT 3 — Capacity Degradation over Cycles
# From metadata — the most important trend in the dataset
# Shows how battery capacity fades over repeated cycles
# Hue = battery_id to see per-battery degradation paths
# ============================================================

discharge_meta = df[df['type'] == 'discharge'].copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    data=discharge_meta,
    x='test_id',
    y='Capacity',
    hue='battery_id',
    alpha=0.6,
    s=15,
    legend=False  # too many batteries for a clean legend
)
plt.title('Battery Capacity Degradation Over Cycles (all batteries)')
plt.xlabel('Cycle Number (test_id)')
plt.ylabel('Capacity (Ah)')
plt.axhline(y=1.4, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label='EOL threshold (1.4 Ah)')
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [12]:
# ============================================================
# SCATTER PLOT 4 — Capacity Degradation with hue = ambient_temperature
# Same degradation plot but now colored by temperature group
# Reveals whether cold or warm batteries degrade differently
# ============================================================

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.scatterplot(
    data=discharge_meta,
    x='test_id',
    y='Capacity',
    hue='ambient_temperature',
    palette='coolwarm',
    alpha=0.6,
    s=15
)
plt.title('Capacity Degradation Over Cycles (hue = ambient temperature)')
plt.xlabel('Cycle Number (test_id)')
plt.ylabel('Capacity (Ah)')
plt.axhline(y=1.4, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, label='EOL threshold (1.4 Ah)')
plt.legend(title='Ambient Temp (°C)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
